rulings.Case Title: FRANK ALIOTO et al VS. WILLIAM F. GARLOCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion To Compel And Order For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 17, 2012, Line 25, DEFENDANT HAMILTON, RICCI & ASSOCIATES, INC. Ntc And Motion To Compel And Order For Sanctions - As to both motions, the court adopts in full the recommendation of the Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne as follows: Each plaintiff shall deliver to counsel for Hamilton Ricci no later than 5pm on January 17 verified responses to each request for admission in sets one and two and, to the extent that such response is other than an unqualified admission, each plaintiff shall deliver at the same time a response to the corresponding form interrogatories. Any failure to comply with the foregoing will result in deemed admissions. The depositions of Frank and Rosemary Alioto shall occur during the week of January 16-20. All plaintiffs will provide verifications to their responses to the set one document requests by January 16. Plaintiff and their counsel shall pay sanctions of $2000. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN CRAIG HIGLEY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 17, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - The motion for summary judgment is denied and the alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the cause of action for dangerous condition of public property in violation of Government Code section 835 and granted as to all other claims. Plaintiff concedes the lack of merit of his common law claims. rulings.Case Title: BARBARA BARTINDALE et al VS. PENSCO TRUST COMPANY, A NEW HAMPSHIRE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 17, 2012, Line 10, DEFENDANT PENSCO TRUST CO., INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - Motion for summary judgment is granted. Nothing in the Custodial Agreement obligated defendant to perfect the ownership interests of plaintiffs' investments or confirm that the transfer documents were signed and recorded. Nor was defendant required under the Custodial Agreement to notify plaintiffs that defendant had not received endorsed copies of notes or deeds. Interpreting the Custodial Agreement in the way that plaintiffs advocate would impermissibly reallocate the parties' responsibilities et forth in the Custodial Agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MECHANICS BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. JOHN T. KONTRABECKI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deft John T Konbrabecki, Trustee, To Appear For Deposition And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 17, 2012, Line 1. PLAINTIFF MECHANICS BANK'S Motion To Compel Defendant John T Konbrabecki, Trustee, To Appear For Deposition And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: OHIO STATE LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. EUPHEMIA FELICITAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Judgment Of Interpleader
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 17, 2012, Line 19, PLAINTIFF OHIO STATE LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY Ntc Of Motion For Judgment Of Interpleader is Granted except that plaintiff is awarded $3000 in fees and costs. Notwithstanding the expertise of its counsel, given the modest amount interpleaded, plaintiff was under an obligation to keep fees and costs to the smallest sum possible. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If no one contests the tentative ruling, the court will sign the proposed judgment submitted with the moving papers.

rulings.Case Title: MELANIE GRAYSMITH VS. CONVERSE INTERNATION SCHOOL OF LANGUAGES SAN et al

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If no one contests the tentative ruling, the court will sign the order submitted with the moving papers. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: 690 MARKET CLUB OWNERS ASSOCIATION VS. WINNIE C. ROSS AKA WINNIE CHU ROSS AKA WINIE CHU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 17, 2012, Line 21, DEFENDANT WINNIE ROSS MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: MINDY FREILE VS. TIM LINCECUM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons And Req. For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 17, 2012, Line 23, DEFENDANT TIM LINCECUM'S MOTION To Quash Service Of Summons IS Granted. Defendant was not properly served. Moreover, the prior order was not intended to preclude the re-filing of the motion to quash on proper notice. Judge Kahn's clear recollection is that, at the time he signed the prior order, counsel for plaintiff acknowledged this very point and thus the positions taken in the opposition papers lack merit. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK
